data.title: Architect-Foundation Guided Mode & UX Improvements
data.description:
## Context
A non-technical user — such as a product manager running /dso:architect-foundation after onboarding — is asked engineering-level questions about enforcement preferences, anti-pattern detection, and ADR generation, then must confirm every file write individually with "Does this look right?" Non-technical users cannot meaningfully answer enforcement questions, and all users experience unnecessary friction from per-file confirmation. ADRs are an engineering best practice that should always be generated.

## Success Criteria
- /dso:architect-foundation accepts an --auto flag; when set, the skill reads project-understanding.md to infer stack and architecture signals and selects recommended defaults — the same defaults the skill would suggest to a user who answered "I don't know, what do you recommend?" to every engineering question. It then presents a single summary of what it will configure
- In --auto mode, diffs against existing files (CLAUDE.md, ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md) are always shown before the single confirmation prompt — "no per-file confirmation" means no individual "Does this look right?" per file, not "no diff visibility". After one confirmation, all files are written
- ADRs are always generated for architectural decisions made during the current session. On re-invocation, the skill detects all existing artifacts produced by a previous architect-foundation run and does not duplicate or overwrite them — this idempotency applies to ADRs, ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md, and all other generated files
- In standard mode (without --auto), file writes are batched: the skill presents a summary listing all files it intends to write with a one-line description of each, gets one confirmation, then writes them all. Phase 2 per-recommendation accept/reject is preserved unchanged
- When re-invoked on a project with existing architect-foundation artifacts, the skill preserves all existing content and appends new recommendations that don't duplicate existing ones — append-only merge, no deep merge or overwrite of customized content. The user is shown a diff of proposed additions
- After implementation, run architect-foundation --auto on a Nava template test project with pre-existing enforcement artifacts, then run without --auto on an equivalent fresh template project. Verify: (a) --auto completes with exactly one confirmation prompt, (b) standard mode shows one batched summary, (c) ADRs are present in both cases, (d) re-run on --auto project does not overwrite customized ARCH_ENFORCEMENT.md

## Scenario Analysis
- ADR duplication on re-invocation (high): SC3 specifies idempotency across all artifacts
- Diff presentation skipped in --auto mode (critical): SC2 explicitly requires diffs before confirmation
- --auto re-run overwrites customized enforcement (critical): SC5 specifies append-only merge

## Dependencies
None — modifies /dso:architect-foundation SKILL.md only.

## Approach
Modify /dso:architect-foundation SKILL.md to add --auto flag parsing, remove the ADR generation question, restructure the file-write flow from per-file confirmation to batched summary confirmation, define recommended defaults for all engineering questions, and add append-only merge logic for re-invocation safety.
